title: Understanding Shear Deformation
text: Shear deformation occurs when a force is applied parallel to the surface of a material, causing it to change shape. In this problem, the wind applies a force at the top of the flagpole, causing it to bend sideways.

title: Shear Modulus and Deformation
text: The shear modulus (G) is a measure of a material's ability to resist shear deformation. It is defined as the ratio of shear stress to shear strain. In this problem, the shear modulus is given as $$2.5\times{10}^{10}$$ N/m².

title: Calculating Shear Stress
text: Shear stress (τ) is calculated using the formula $$τ=\frac{F}{A}$$, where F is the force applied and A is the cross-sectional area. For a cylinder, $$A=\pi \left(\frac{d}{2}\right)^2$$, where $$d$$ is the diameter.

title: Calculate the Cross-Sectional Area
text: Given the diameter of the solid cylinder is $$4.00$$ cm, calculate the cross-sectional area.

title: Determine the Shear Stress
text: Using the force of $$900.0$$ N and the cross-sectional area calculated, determine the shear stress.

title: Calculate Shear Strain
text: Shear strain (γ) is calculated using the formula $$γ=\frac{\tau}{G}$$, where τ is the shear stress and G is the shear modulus.

title: Determine the Sideways Flex
text: The sideways flex (Δx) at the top of the pole can be calculated using the formula $$Δx=\gamma L$$, where L is the length of the pole.
